summary: SCH4U Unit 1 problems ask you to draw a molecule's Lewis structure, use VSEPR to name its shape, and decide whether it is polar. Shape comes from how electron groups spread around the central atom; polarity comes from bond dipoles plus that shape.
formulas: Octet: bonding pairs + lone pairs ≈ 8 e⁻
molecule polar if bond dipoles don't cancel
method: Count the total valence electrons from all atoms.
Place the least electronegative atom in the centre and draw single bonds.
Add lone pairs to complete octets; form double/triple bonds if needed.
Count electron groups on the central atom and apply VSEPR for the shape.
Check bond polarities (ΔEN) and whether they cancel by symmetry to decide molecular polarity.

example.title: Shape and polarity of carbon dioxide
example.find: the molecular shape and whether CO₂ is polar
steps: Carbon is central with two double bonds to oxygen and no lone pairs on C — two electron groups.
Two groups arrange linearly, so the shape is linear (O=C=O, 180°).
Each C=O bond is polar (ΔEN ≈ 0.8), but the two dipoles point in exactly opposite directions.
example.answer: Linear molecule; nonpolar overall (the bond dipoles cancel)
